title: Who Is the Goebbels: Me or Jayalalithaa?
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The printed source was consulted for the English Frontline quotations and their surrounding Tamil, where OCR inserted an isolated Latin-symbol fragment. Genuine English is preserved. Only source-confirmed or unambiguous OCR/page-break errors are repaired in the Tamil reading copy. The canonical Tamil chapter remains unchanged.
paragraphs: Speaking at an election campaign meeting in Thoothukudi, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa said it was in no way fair for Karunanidhi alone to claim credit for Muslim reservation, and that it was she who sowed the seed for minority reservation during her government. Rather than reply myself, let me cite what was said by Professor Kader Mohideen, leader of the Indian Union Muslim League, a close friend of Anna and one who works for Muslims every day, and by Jawahirullah, legislative-party leader of the Manithaneya Makkal Katchi, who allied with Jayalalithaa last time and travelled throughout the State working for her party's victory. Their words will make clear whether reservation for Muslims was granted by the DMK government or by Jayalalithaa.

Speaking to reporters after his party's National Political Advisory Committee meeting on 10 March 2014, Professor Kader Mohideen said: "Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Jayalalithaa is making a statement contrary to truth by claiming that she granted separate reservation to Muslims. This is a blatant falsification of history. It is recorded history that Kalaignar, the DMK president, granted 3.5 per cent separate reservation to Muslims and included Labbais, Dakhnis, Dudekulas, Mapillas and others in the Backward Classes list that made this reservation possible. By replacing 'socially and educationally backward' with 'economically backward' in the AIADMK manifesto, Jayalalithaa follows BJP policy exactly. The Muslim community will therefore never accept her claim."

Mr Jawahirullah said yesterday: "It was under the DMK government that Muslims were given 3.5 per cent reservation. Though I spoke many times in the Assembly asking for it to be increased, the AIADMK government ignored the demand. The AIADMK has no standing to claim that it granted reservation to Muslims." Are the truths stated by these two eminent Muslim leaders enough to show what an enormous fraud Jayalalithaa is, or are they not?

Trying to escape now, Jayalalithaa says Muslim reservation resulted from a measure she took. A wife belongs to the man who tied the thaali, not to every man who merely came to see her as a prospective bride. If such a man claimed she belonged to him, the whole village would come to kick him!

At Thoothukudi, Jayalalithaa also tried to escape by claiming that the AIADMK had never sent people for kar seva. Do the people of Tamil Nadu not know Jayalalithaa's connection with kar seva and how she behaved then? Do you know the headline of Dinamani's report of 24 November 1992? "Kar Seva: Jayalalithaa Insists." It reported the speech Jayalalithaa delivered as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister at the National Integration Council meeting in Delhi.

In that speech, she said: "It is not appropriate for minorities to pursue their interests against the background of political, historical and social structures in a manner that affects the ordinary rights and privileges available to the majority under the Constitution. The majority too must be allowed to enjoy its rights like the minorities. Applying this specifically to the Ayodhya issue means permitting construction to be completed as Hindus desire on the land acquired by the Uttar Pradesh government.

"I am aware that the Supreme Court's directions to the Uttar Pradesh government and the cases pending in the High Court have created a legal situation that makes it difficult to undertake kar seva or any other action on the acquired land. If we accept that construction at that site is not prohibited, the Union and Uttar Pradesh governments must take the necessary steps to remove the legal obstacles. This National Integration Council must decide to create an atmosphere conducive to conducting kar seva. Every necessary step must be taken to approach the courts for permission. To state my request very briefly: the conditions necessary for kar seva on the land acquired by the Uttar Pradesh government must be created."

When this was reported everywhere at the time, will people now believe her if she goes to Thoothukudi and says the AIADMK never sent anyone for kar seva, like trying to hide a whole pumpkin in a serving of rice?

It was not Dinamani alone. Frontline of 1 January 1993 quoted her in English: "I am aware that the Supreme Court directions to the Uttar Pradesh Government and the pendency of the cases in the High Court have created a legal situation making it difficult for the Kar Sewa"; and, "This august body will, therefore, have to take a decision on creating a congenial atmosphere for the conduct of the Kar Sewa. All steps will have to be taken to move the Courts to permit Kar Sewa." Will Jayalalithaa say that report too was fabricated?

A Tamil Nadu Vishwa Hindu Parishad poster responding to the Chief Minister's National Integration Council speech read: "Thank you! Thank you! Thank you to the Honourable Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Selvi Jayalalithaa for reflecting the feelings of crores of Hindus by speaking in support of Ayodhya kar seva at the National Integration Council meeting!"

That is not all. In an interview published by The Times of India on 3 December 1992, Gulab Singh Parihar, secretary of the Vishwa Hindu Parishad in Uttar Pradesh, where the kar seva was held, said: "Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Jayalalithaa has sent more than one thousand AIADMK members for the kar seva at Ayodhya. She has also promised several forms of assistance." In which newspaper did Jayalalithaa leap up at the time and deny this, saying the report was false and that she had sent no one? Is she prepared to say? She treated that report as praise then. If she now says, for Muslim votes, that she sent no one, will Muslims believe her?

When the late A. K. A. Abdul Samad, Tamil Nadu president of the Indian Union Muslim League, charged at the time that AIADMK volunteers had participated in the Ayodhya kar seva, did Jayalalithaa deny it?

Do the people of Tamil Nadu not see how Jayalalithaa torments Muslims? A poor senior IPS officer belonging to the Muslim community is being persecuted merely because he held an important post under the DMK government. He has been kept under suspension throughout the three years since this government came to office. Indeed, the government has ordered that he may not even come to Chennai and must remain at Mandapam near Rameswaram. Do Tamil Nadu's Muslims not know this? Under the DMK government, not only was this officer given major responsibility; when a file came to me seeking an able special officer for the World Classical Tamil Conference, I appointed the senior officer Alauddin. Fortunately, he has now retired. Otherwise, he too might have been victimised.

When BJP members collected lakhs of signatures in Tamil Nadu demanding that a Ram temple be built precisely where the Babri Masjid was demolished and submitted the petition to the Governor, the BJP announced that AIADMK MLAs K. R. Muruganantham, Ramesh, Panneerselvam and Govindaraj had signed it. Jayalalithaa, who denies me today, did not deny it then. Can Muslims be deceived by talk of this kind?
Dina Thanthi, which today publishes Jayalalithaa's speech in banner type, carried an interview with her on 23 July 2004. Here is one question from it.
Reporter: "What is your view on granting reservation to Muslims, a minority community?"

Jayalalithaa: "Muslims alone are not minorities. There are Christians, Parsis and Buddhists. As far as Tamil Nadu is concerned, Muslims and Christians are equal. If separate reservation is granted to Muslims, Christians will demand it tomorrow. Therefore, doing so is impossible. Under the Constitution, they already enjoy many concessions. The majority community does not enjoy all those concessions. No further concessions can be given to minority communities that already enjoy concessions."
Did this very Jayalalithaa say that, or did she not? Does she deny this too? Now tell me: who is Tamil Nadu's Goebbels? Me or Jayalalithaa?
With affection,
M. K.
17 March 2014
